lesson.menu_desc: New block -- open a meeting, set the agenda and share opinions -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Opening the Meeting -- Agenda & Opinions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Percival JR | Aula 11 -- Opening the Meeting

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone. Thank you for coming to today's meeting. We have three topics on the agenda. First, the safety project. Second, the new supplier. And third, the team schedule. Let's start with the first topic. I would like to hear your opinion on the safety project. Please feel free to agree or disagree. At the end, we are going to make a decision together. If something is not clear, just ask me to clarify.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. I have a quick message about tomorrow's meeting. I would like to suggest one change to the agenda. I think we should talk about the budget first, because it is the most important topic. In my opinion, the supplier decision can wait. I do not want to disagree with your plan, but the budget is urgent. Can you clarify the start time? Thank you, and see you tomorrow.

extra_audio.text: First, Percival welcomes everyone to the meeting. Then, he presents the three topics on the agenda. After that, he asks the team for their opinion. Later, the team agrees or disagrees with each idea. Finally, they make a decision together.
